items.title: My Farm Journal Launches to Create a More Connected Customer Experience
items.author: Farm Journal
items.content:
– Farm Journal today announced the launch of My Farm Journal, a new customer portal designed to create a more connected and personalized experience across the company’s network of brands, events, and business solutions. 

My Farm Journal serves as a centralized hub where producers and agribusiness professionals can manage subscriptions, access exclusive content and insights, register for events and webinars, and engage with resources tailored to their interests and operations – all from a single account. 

As Farm Journal continues to expand its capabilities in media, events, data, intelligence, and business solutions, My Farm Journal represents the next step for deepening customer engagement. 

“The trust we’ve established with producers over generations has always been rooted in delivering valuable information, insights, and connections,” said Prescott Shibles, CEO. “My Farm Journal strengthens those relationships by creating a more cohesive and personalized experience across our network of brands and making it easier for customers to access resources relevant to them.” 

The launch represents another step in Farm Journal’s continued investment in customer experience, digital capabilities, and stronger connections between producers and the information, services, and experiences that support their success. 

“Farm Journal has evolved far beyond a traditional media company and will continue to grow as an information company,” Shibles continues. “My Farm Journal creates the foundation for how we will connect customers with our brands, data & insights, events, business solutions, and future innovations in the years ahead.” 

Farm Journal is the nation’s leading business information and media company serving the agricultural market. The company serves the row crop, livestock, produce and retail sectors through branded websites, eNewsletters and phone apps; business magazines; live events including conferences and tradeshows; nationally broadcast television and radio programs; and an array of data-driven, paid information products. Farm Journal owns the online equipment marketplace, Machinery Pete LLC. Trust In Food is a Farm Journal initiative dedicated to accelerating the adoption of conservation practices and regenerative agriculture in ways that work for producers and enhance connection to consumers. In 2010, the company established the non-profit, public charity, Farm Journal Foundation, dedicated to sustaining agriculture’s ability to meet the vital needs of a growing population through education and empowerment.
